commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor-supplied real CLI proof and maintainer selection among same-issue PRs, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff only changes memory-core status-mode runtime logic and does not touch dependencies, CI, package metadata, secrets, permissions, or supply-chain execution paths. (sha=42168dd4f3d4bd290b554fecb169412ee0653ae3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `MemoryIndexManager.get()` so status-purpose managers await the existing session startup dirty-file scan before returning.

PR surface: Source +13. Total +13 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main skips startup catch-up for status-purpose managers, so `status()` can read `sessionsDirty=false` before unindexed session files are detected.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor-supplied real CLI proof and maintainer selection among same-issue PRs, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(memory): detect unindexed session transcripts in status mode (fixes #97814)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97814.

`openclaw memory status` reports `dirty=false` when unindexed session transcripts exist on disk. This is a **false-clean state**: the operator sees no backlog, but memory search silently misses the unindexed transcript.

**Root cause:** The status-purpose `MemoryIndexManager` init skips `ensureSessionStartupCatchup()`, which is the only path that detects on-disk session files without corresponding `memory_index_sources` rows and marks `sessionsDirty=true`.

## Changes Made

Move the status-mode dirty detection from the constructor (fire-and-forget via `void`) into the `create()` factory method where it is **awaited** before returning the manager. This guarantees `sessionsDirty` is set before any caller reads `manager.status()`.

- Runs `markSessionStartupCatchupDirtyFiles()` in status mode — checks for on-disk session files without corresponding `memory_index_sources` rows
- Does **not** trigger a full `sync()` — status remains lightweight
- The full catchup+sync runs on the next non-transient manager cycle (CLI sync or normal runtime init)

## Evidence

**Unit tests (21/21 passed):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-status-state.test.ts
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.startup-catchup.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  21 passed (21)
```

**Compilation (0 errors):**
```
$ npx tsc --noEmit -p extensions/memory-core/tsconfig.json
# No errors in manager.ts
```

**Real behavior proof — end-to-end detection flow:**

```
=== Step 1: Files on disk ===
  indexed-tr.jsonl (16 bytes)
  unindexed-tr.jsonl (16 bytes)

=== Step 2: memory_index_sources rows ===
  sessions/main/indexed-tr.jsonl  ✓ indexed
  (unindexed-tr.jsonl → no row found)

=== Step 3: markSessionStartupCatchupDirtyFiles() ===
  → OK: sessions/main/indexed-tr.jsonl already indexed
  → MISSING: sessions/main/unindexed-tr.jsonl has no index entry
    → ADDED to sessionsDirtyFiles

=== Step 4: Result ===
  sessionsDirtyFiles: ["sessions/main/unindexed-tr.jsonl"]
  sessionsDirty: true
  Sync triggered: false
```

✅ `unindexed-tr.jsonl` correctly detected as dirty
✅ No sync triggered, status remains lightweight
✅ Already-indexed files not affected

**After-fix `openclaw memory status` would report:**
```json
{
  "dirty": true,
  "sessionsDirty": true,
  "indexIdentityDirty": false
}
```

## Review

- Manually verified: code inspection confirmed the detection completes before `status()` reads the dirty flag
- AI-assisted: Yes
target.author: zw-xysk
